result:
Done. I implemented the three missing operations in `CsvRow.cs`, the project builds cleanly, and a round-trip check confirms the behavior.

- **`EscapeField`** returns the field verbatim unless `NeedsQuoting` holds, in which case it doubles internal quotes and wraps the result in double-quotes.
- **`JoinRow`** escapes each field and joins with commas; the empty list yields the empty string.
- **`SplitRow`** walks the line field by field: a field starting with `"` is parsed to the next un-doubled quote (with `""` contributing one literal quote, and commas/CR/LF inside kept as content); otherwise the field runs to the next comma or end of line. The empty line parses to a single empty field, and a trailing comma produces a final empty field.

`NeedsQuoting` is untouched, and everything is pure computation with no I/O or global state. I verified the round-trip property (`SplitRow(JoinRow(fields)) == fields`) against cases including embedded commas, quotes, doubled quotes, CR/LF, empty fields, and trailing empties using a throwaway console program in /tmp (since test files aren't allowed in the project) — all passed, and the temp program has been removed. Used 2 of the 10 build cycles.
